author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter V. 24–30.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Upon the discourses delivered yesterday and the day before, follows the Gospel lesson of to-day, which we must endeavor to expound in due course, not indeed proportionably to its importance, but according to our ability: both because you take in, not according to the bountifulness of the gushing fountain, but according to your moderate capacity; and we too speak into your ears, not so much as the fountain gives forth, but so much as we are able to take in we convey into your minds,—the matter itself working more fruitfully in your hearts than we in your ears.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For a great matter is treated of, not by great masters, nay, rather by very small; but He who, being great, for our sakes became small, gives us hope and confidence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if we were not encouraged by Him, and invited to understand Him; if He abandoned us as contemptible, since we were not able to partake His divinity if He did not partake our mortality and come to us to speak His gospel to us; if He had not willed to partake with us what in us is abject and most small,—then we might think that He who took on Himself our smallness, had not been willing to bestow on us His own greatness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This I have said lest any should blame us as over-bold in handling these matters, or despair of himself that he should be able to understand, by God’s gift, what the Son of God has deigned to speak to him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore what He has deigned to speak to us, we ought to believe that He meant us to understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if we do not understand, He, being asked, gives understanding, who gave His Word unasked.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lo, what these secrets of His words are, consider well.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whoso heareth my word, and believeth on Him that sent me, hath eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely we are all striving after eternal life: and He saith, “Whoso heareth my word, and believeth Him that sent me, hath eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, would He have us hear His word, and yet would He not have us understand it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, if in hearing and believing is eternal life, much more in understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the action of piety is faith, the fruit of faith understanding, that we may come to eternal life, when there will be no reading of Gospel to us; but after all pages of reading and the voice of reader and preacher have been removed out of the way, He, who has at this time dispensed to us the gospel, will Himself appear to all that are His, now present with Him with purged heart and in an immortal body never more to die, cleansing and enlightening them, now living and seeing how that “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore let us consider at this time who we are, and ponder whom we hear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ is God, and He is speaking with men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He would have them to apprehend Him, let Him make them capable; He would have them see Him, let Him open their eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not, however, without cause that He speaks to us, but because that is true which He promises to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Whoso heareth my words,” saith He, “and believeth Him that sent me, hath eternal life, and shall not come into judgment, but is passed from death unto life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where, when do we come from death to life, that we come not into judgment?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this life there is a passing from death to life; in this life, which is not yet life, there is a passing hence from death unto life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is that passing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Whoso heareth my words,” He said, “and believeth Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Observing these, thou believest and passest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And does a man pass while standing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Evidently; for in body he stands, in mind he passes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where was he, whence he should pass, and whither does he pass?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He passes from death to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look at a man standing, in whom all that is here said may happen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He stands, he hears, perhaps he did not believe, by hearing he believes: a little before he did not believe, just now he believes; he has made a passage, as it were, from the region of unbelief to the region of faith, by motion of the heart, not of the body, by a motion into the better; because they who again abandon faith move into the worse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, in this life, which, just as I have said, is not yet life, there is a passing from death to life, so that there may not be a coming into judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why did I say that it is not yet life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If this were life, the Lord would not have said to a certain man, “If thou wilt come into life, keep the commandments.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He saith not to him, If thou wilt come into eternal life; He did not add eternal, but said only life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore this life is not to be named life, because it is not a true life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is true life, but that which is eternal life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the apostle speaking to Timothy, when he says, “Charge them that are rich in this world, not to be high-minded, nor to trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God, who giveth us all things richly to enjoy; let them do good, be rich in good works, ready to distribute, to communicate.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why does he say this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what follows: “Let them lay up in store for themselves a good foundation for the time to come, that they may lay hold of the true life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they ought to lay up for themselves a good foundation for the time to come, in order to lay hold of the true life, surely this in which they were is a false life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For why shouldest thou desire to lay hold of the true, if thou hast the true already?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is the true to be laid hold of?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There must then be a departing from the false.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And by what way must be the departing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear, believe; and thou makest the passage from death into life, and comest not into judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this, “and thou comest not into judgment”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who will be better than the Apostle Paul, who saith, “We must all appear before the judgment-seat of Christ, that every one may there receive what he has done in the body, whether it be good or evil”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Paul saith, “We must all appear before the judgment-seat of Christ;” and darest thou promise to thyself that thou shalt not come into judgment?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be it far from me, sayest thou, that I should dare promise this to myself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I believe Him that doth promise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Saviour speaks, the Truth promises, Himself said to me, “Whoso heareth my words, and believeth Him that sent me, hath eternal life, and makes a passage from death unto life, and shall not come into judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I then have heard the words of my Lord, and I have believed; so now, when I was an unbeliever, I became a believer; even as He warned me, I passed from death to life, I come not into judgment; not by my presumption, but by His promise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does Paul, however, speak contrary to Christ, the servant against his Lord, the disciple against his Master, the man against God; so that, when the Lord saith, “Whoso heareth and believeth, passeth from death to life,” the apostle should say, “We must all appear before the judgment-seat of Christ”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Otherwise, if he comes not into judgment who appears before the judgment-seat, I know not how to understand it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord our God then reveals it, and by His Scriptures puts us in mind how it may be understood when judgment is spoken of.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I exhort you, therefore, to give attention.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sometimes judgment means punishment, sometimes it means discrimination.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: According to that mode of speech in which judgment means discrimination, “we must all appear before the judgment-seat of Christ that” a man “may there receive what things he has done in the body, whether it be good or ill.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this same is a discrimination, to distribute good things to the good, evil things to the evil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if judgment were always to be taken in a bad sense, the psalm would not say, “Judge me, O God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps some one is surprised when he hears one say, “Judge me, O God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For man is wont to say, “Forgive me, O God;” “Spare me, O God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is it that says, “Judge me, O God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sometimes in the psalm this very verse even is placed in the pause, to be given out by the reader and responded by the people.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does it not perhaps strike some man’s heart so much that he is afraid to sing and to say to God, “Judge me, O God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet the people sing it with confidence, and do not imagine that they wish an evil thing in that which they have learned from the divine word; even if they do not well understand it, they believe that what they sing is something good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet even the psalm itself has not left a man without an insight into the meaning of it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, going on, it shows in the words that follow what kind of judgment it spoke of; that it is not one of condemnation, but of discrimination.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For saith it, “Judge me, O God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means “Judge me, O God, and discern my cause from an unholy nation”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: According to this judgment of discerning, then, “we must all appear before the judgment-seat of Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But again, according to the judgment of condemnation, “Whoso heareth my words,” saith He, “and believeth Him that sent me, hath eternal life, and shall not come into judgment, but makes a passage from death to life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is “shall not come into judgment?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Shall not come into condemnation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us prove from the Scriptures that judgment is put where punishment is understood; although also in this very passage, a little further on, you will hear the same term judgment put for nothing else than for condemnation and punishment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet the apostle says in a certain place, writing to those who abused the body, what the faithful among you know; and because they abused it, they were chastised by the scourge of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he says to them, “Many among you are weak and sickly, and deeply sleep.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For many therefore even died.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he went on: “For if we judged ourselves, we should not be judged by the Lord;” that is, if we reproved ourselves, we should not be reproved by the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But when we are judged, we are chastened by the Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are therefore those who are judged here according to punishment, that they may be spared there; there are those who are spared here, that they may be the more abundantly tormented there; and there are those to whom the very punishments are meted out without the scourge of punishment, if they be not corrected by the scourge of God; that, since here they have despised the Father that scourgeth, they may there feel the Judge that punisheth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore there is a judgment into which God, that is, the Son of God, will in the end send the devil and his angels, and all the unbelieving and ungodly with him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To this judgment, he who, now believing, passes from death unto life, shall not come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, lest thou shouldest think that by believing thou art not to die according to the flesh, or lest, understanding it carnally, thou shouldest say to thyself, “My Lord has said to me, Whoso heareth my words, and believeth Him that sent me, is passed from death to life: I then have believed, I am not to die;” be assured that thou shalt pay that penalty, death, which thou owest by the punishment of Adam.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he, in whom we all then were, received this sentence, “Thou shalt surely die;” nor can the divine sentence be made void.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But after thou hast paid the death of the old man, thou shalt be received into the eternal life of the new man, and shalt pass from death to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mean while, make the transition of life now.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is thy life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Faith: “The just doth live by faith.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The unbelievers, what of them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They are dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Among such dead was he, in the body, of whom the Lord says, “Let the dead bury their dead.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, then, even in this life there are dead, and there are living; all live in a sense.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who are dead?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who have not believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who are living?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who have believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is said to the dead by the apostle?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Arise, thou that sleepest.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, quoth an objector, he said sleep, not death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what follows: “Arise, thou that sleepest, and come forth from the dead.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And as if the sleeper said, Whither shall I go?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And Christ shall give thee light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ having enlightened thee, now believing, immediately thou makest a passage from death to life: abide in that to which thou hast passed, and thou shalt not come into judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Himself explains that already, and goes on, “Verily, verily, I say unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In case, because He said “is passed from death to life,” we should understand this of the future resurrection, and willing to show that he who believes is passed, and that to pass from death to life is to pass from unbelief to faith, from injustice to justice, from pride to humility, from hatred to charity, He saith now, “Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour cometh, and now is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What more evident?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God, and they that hear shall live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have already spoken of these dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What think we, my brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are there no dead in this crowd that hear me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who believe and act according to the true faith do live, and are not dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they who either do not believe, or believe as the devils believe, trembling, and living wickedly, confessing the Son of God, and without charity, must rather be esteemed dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This hour, however, is still passing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the hour of which the Lord spoke will not be an hour of the twelve hours of a day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From the time when He spoke even to the present, and even to the end of the world, the same one hour is passing; of which hour John saith in his epistle, “Little children, it is the last hour.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, is now.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whoso is alive, let him live; whoso was dead, let him live; let him hear the voice of the Son of God, who lay dead; let him arise and live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord cried out at the sepulchre of Lazarus, and he that was four days dead arose.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who stank in the grave came forth into the air.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was buried, a stone was laid over him: the voice of the Saviour burst asunder the hardness of the stone; and thy heart is so hard, that Divine Voice does not yet break it!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rise in thy heart; go forth from thy tomb.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou wast lying dead in thy heart as in a tomb, and pressed down by the weight of evil habit as by a stone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rise, and go forth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is Rise, and go forth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Believe and confess.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he that has believed has risen; he that confesses is gone forth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why said we that he who confesses is gone forth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because he was hid before confessing; but when he does confess, he goes forth from darkness to light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And after he has confessed, what is said to the servants?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was said beside the corpse of Lazarus?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Loose him, and let him go.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As it was said to His servants the apostles, “What things ye shall loose on earth, shall be loosed in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The hour cometh, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God; and they that hear shall live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From what source shall they live?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From what life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How do we prove that the source is Christ the life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I am,” saith He, “the way, the truth, and the life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to walk?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I am the way.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou wish not to be deceived?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I am the truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wouldest thou not die?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I am the life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This saith thy Saviour to thee: There is not whither thou mayest go but to me; there is not whereby thou mayest go but by me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore this hour is going on now, this act is clearly taking place, and does not at all cease.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Men who were dead, rise; they pass over to life; at the voice of the Son of God they live; from Him they live, while persevering in the faith of Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Son hath life, whence He has it that they that believe shall live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how hath He?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even as the Father hath.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear Himself saying, “For as the Father hath life in Himself, so also hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Brethren, I shall speak as I shall be able.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For these are those words that perplex the puny understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why has He added, “in Himself”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It would suffice to say, “For as the Father hath life, so also hath He given to the Son to have life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He added, “in Himself:” for the Father “hath life in Himself,” and the Son hath life in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He meant us to understand something in that which He saith, “in Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And here a secret matter is shut up in this word; let there be knocking, that there may be an opening.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O Lord, what is this that Thou hast said?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore hast Thou added, “in Himself”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For did not Paul the apostle, whom Thou madest to live, have life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had, said He.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As for men that were dead to be made alive, and at Thy word to pass unto life by believing; when they shall have passed, will they not have life in Thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They shall have life; for I said also a little before, “Whoso heareth my words, and believeth Him that sent me, hath eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore those that believe in Thee have life; and Thou hast not said, “in themselves.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when Thou speakest of the Father, “even as the Father hath life in Himself;” again, when Thou speakest of Thyself, Thou saidst, “So also hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even as He hath, so gave He to have.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where hath He?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where gave He to have?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where hath Paul life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not in himself, but in Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where hast thou, believer?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not in thyself, but in Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see whether the apostle says this: “Now I live; but not I, but Christ liveth in me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Our life, as ours, that is, of our own personal will, will be only evil, sinful, unrighteous; but the life in us that is good is from God, not from ourselves; it is given to us by God, not by ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Christ hath life in Himself, as the Father hath, because He is the Word of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With Him, it is not the case that He liveth now ill, now well; but as for man, he liveth now ill, now well.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who was living ill, was in his own life; he who is living well, is passed to the life of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art made a partaker of life; thou wast not that which thou hast received, but wast one who received: but it is not so with the Son of God as if at first He was without life, and then received life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if thus He received life, He would not have it in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, indeed, what is in Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That He should Himself be the very life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I may perhaps declare that matter more plainly still.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One lights a candle: that candle, for example, so far as regards the little flame which shines there—that fire has light in itself; but thine eyes, which lay idle and saw nothing, in the absence of the candle, now have light also, but not in themselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Further, if they turn away from the candle, they are made dark; if they turn to it, they are illumined.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But certainly that fire shines so long as it exists: if thou wouldst take the light from it, thou dost also at the same time extinguish it; for without the light it cannot remain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Christ is light inextinguishable and co-eternal with the Father, always bright, always shining, always burning: for if He were not burning, would it be said in the psalm, “Nor is there any that can hide himself from his heat?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But thou wast cold in thy sin; thou turnest that thou mayest become warm; if thou wilt turn away, thou wilt become cold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In thy sin thou wast dark; thou turnest in order to be enlightened; if thou turnest away, thou wilt become dark.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, because in thyself thou wast darkness, when thou shalt be enlightened, thou wilt be light, though in the light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For saith the apostle, “Ye were once darkness, but now light in the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When he had said, “but now light,” he added, “in the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore in thyself darkness, “light in the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what way “light”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because by participation of that light thou art light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou wilt depart from the light by which thou art enlightened, thou returnest to thy darkness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not so Christ, not so the Word of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “As the Father hath life in Himself, so hath He given also to the Son to have life in Himself;” so that He lives, not by participation, but unchangeably, and is altogether Himself life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “So hath He given also to the Son to have life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even as He hath, so has He given.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the difference?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the one gave, the other received.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was He already in being when He received?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are we to understand that Christ was at any time in being without light, when Himself is the wisdom of the Father, of which it is said, “It is the brightness of the eternal light?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore what is said, “gave to the Son,” is such as if it were said, “begat the Son;” for by begetting He gave.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As He gave Him to be, so He gave Him to be life, so also gave Him to be life in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is that, to be life in Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not to need life from elsewhere, but to be Himself the plenitude of life, out of which others believing should have life while they lived.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Hath given Him,” then, “to have life in Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hath given as to whom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As to His own Word, as to Him who “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Afterwards, because He was made man, what gave He to Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And hath given Him authority to execute judgment, because He is the Son of man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that He is the Son of God, “As the Father hath life in Himself, so also hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself;” in that He is the Son of man, “He hath given Him authority of executing judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is what I ex plained to you yesterday, my beloved, that in the judgment man will be seen, but God will not be seen; but after the judgment, God will be seen by those who have prevailed in the judgment, but by the wicked He will not be seen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, therefore, the man will be seen in the judgment in that form in which He will so come as He ascended, for that reason He had said above, “The Father judgeth not any man, but hath given all judgment to the Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He repeats the same thing also in this place, when He says, “And hath given Him authority of executing judgment, because He is the Son of man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if thou wert to say, “hath given Him authority of executing judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what way?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When He had not that authority of executing judgment?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God;” since “all things were made by Him,” did He not already have authority of executing judgment?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yes, but according to this, I say, “He gave Him authority of executing judgment, because He is the Son of man:” according to this, He received authority of judging “because He is the Son of man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in that He is the Son of God, He always had this authority.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that was crucified, received; He who was in death, is in life: the Word of God never was in death, but is always in life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, therefore, as to a resurrection, perhaps some one of us was saying: Behold, we have risen; he who hears Christ, and believes, and is passed from death to life, also will not come into judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The hour cometh, and now is, that whoso heareth the voice of the Son of God shall live: he was dead, he has heard; behold, he doth rise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this that is said, that there is to be a resurrection afterwards?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Spare thyself, do not hasten the sentence, lest thou hurry after it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is, indeed, this resurrection which comes to pass now; unbelievers were dead, the unrighteous were dead; the righteous live, they pass from the death of unbelief to the life of faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But do not thence believe that there will not be a resurrection afterwards of the body; believe that there will be a resurrection of the body also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For hear what follows after the declaration of this resurrection which is by faith, lest any should think this to be the only resurrection, or fall into that desperation and error of men who perverted the thoughts of others, “saying that the resurrection is past already,” of whom the apostle saith, “and they overthrow the faith of some.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I believe that they were saying to them such words as these: “Behold, when the Lord saith, And he that believeth in me is passed from death unto life;” the resurrection has already taken place in believing men, who were before unbelievers: how can a second resurrection be meant?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thanks to our Lord God, He supports the wavering, directs the perplexed, confirms the doubting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what follows, now that thou hast not whereof to make to thyself the darkness of death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou hast believed, believe the whole.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What whole, sayest thou, am I to believe?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what He saith: “Marvel not at this,” namely, that He gave to the Son authority of making judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I say, in the end of the world, saith He.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How in the end?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Do not marvel at this; for the hour cometh.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here He has not said, “and now is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In reference to that resurrection of faith, what did He say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The hour cometh, and now is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In reference to that resurrection which He intimates there will be of dead bodies, He said, “The hour cometh;” He has not said, “and now is,” because it is to come in the end of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whence, sayest thou, dost thou prove to me that He spoke about the resurrection itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou hear patiently, thou wilt presently prove it to thyself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us go on then: “Marvel not at this; for the hour cometh, in which all that are in the graves.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What more evident than this resurrection?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A while ago, He had not said, “they that are in the graves,” but, “The dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God; and they that hear shall live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has not said, some shall live, others shall be damned; because all who believe shall live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what does He say concerning the graves?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “All that are in the graves shall hear His voice, and shall come forth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “shall hear and live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if they have lived wickedly, and lay in the graves, they shall rise to death, not to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see, then, who shall come forth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Although, a little before, the dead by hearing and believing did live, there was no distinction there made: it was not said, The dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God; and when they shall have heard, some shall live, and some shall be damned; but, “all that hear shall live:” because they that believe shall live, they that have charity shall live, and none of them shall die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But concerning the graves, “They shall hear His voice, and come forth: they that have done well, to the resurrection of life; they that have done ill, to the resurrection of judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the judgment, that punishment of which He had said a while before, “Whoso believeth in me is passed from death to life,” and shall not come into judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I cannot of myself do anything; as I hear I judge, and my judgment is just.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If as Thou hearest Thou judgest, of whom dost Thou hear?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If of the Father, yet surely “the Father judgeth not any man, but hath given all judgment to the Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When dost Thou, being in a manner the Father’s herald, declare what Thou hearest?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I speak what I hear, because what the Father is, that I am: for, indeed, speaking is my function; because I am the Father’s Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this Christ says to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thereupon, of thine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is “As I hear I judge,” but “As I am”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in what manner does Christ hear?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us inquire, brethren, I beg of you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does Christ hear of the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How doth the Father speak to Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Undoubtedly, if He speaks to Him, He uses words to Him; for every one who says something to any one, says it by a word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How doth the Father speak to the Son, seeing that the Son is the Father’s Word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whatever the Father says to us, He says it by His Word: the Word of the Father is the Son; by what other word, then, doth He speak to the Word Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God is one, has one Word, contains all things in one Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does that mean, then, “As I hear, I judge?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as I am of the Father, so I judge.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “my judgment is just.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If Thou doest nothing of Thyself, O Lord Jesus, as carnal men think; if Thou doest nothing of Thyself, how didst Thou say a while before, “So also the Son quickeneth whom He will”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just now Thou sayest, Of myself I do nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what does the Son declare, but that He is of the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that is of the Father is not of Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the Son were of Himself, He would not be the Son: He is of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That the Father is, is not of the Son; that the Son is, is of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Equal to the Father; but yet the Son of the Father, not the Father of the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because I seek not my own will, but the will of Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Only Son saith, “I seek not my own will,” and yet men desire to do their own will!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To such a degree does He who is equal to the Father humble Himself; and to such a degree does He extol Himself, who lies in the lowest depth, and cannot rise except a hand is reached to Him!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us then do the will of the Father, the will of the Son, the will of the Holy Ghost; because of this Trinity there is one will, one power, one majesty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet for that reason saith the Son, “I came not to do mine own will, but the will of Him that sent me;” because Christ is not of Himself, but of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what He had that He might appear as a man, He assumed of the creature which He himself formed.
